mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals accelerating labor market recovery, which drives upward earnings revisions and generates positive short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive medium-term equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during early post-shock recovery phases lifts cyclical equity valuations as capacity utilization improves and supply constraints ease, generating positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth decelerates for two consecutive months, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52 percent over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth momentum translates directly into positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as institutional investors adjust portfolios ahead of scheduled earnings announcements.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates over a 10-year sample (correlation coefficient < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation exceeding a 0.4 percent month-over-month threshold forces tighter monetary policy expectations, which compresses equity valuation multiples and drives negative quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite sustained inflation above the threshold, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.20 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and unemployment decline confirm labor market normalization, reducing wage-price spiral fears while supporting consumer spending, which drives positive 20-day forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect becomes negative or statistically insignificant when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes, or forward returns underperform the market by more than 100 basis points in 60 percent of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, rising retail sales often precede margin compression and aggressive rate hikes, triggering short-term equity drawdowns over the next 5 trading days as risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 60 percent of late-cycle instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for dividend yield changes.
